descriptions: Hells Angels waged a war against Outcasts in the late 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: During the late 1990s, the Hells Angels waged a two-year war against the Outcasts biker gang, which was centred in London and East Anglia.

descriptions: Lone Legion is a member of the A-Team alliance with Outcasts MC.
edges.evidence_quote: According to media and Police the club is a member of the "A-Team", an alliance between several NZ biker groups including the Outcasts MC, the Epitaph Riders MC, the Forty-Fives MC, the Southern Vikings MC, Satan's Slaves MC, Sinn Fein MC and the Lost Breed MC .

descriptions: Outcasts attempted to absorb Lost Tribe, sparking conflict with Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The dispute began when the Outcasts tried to absorb a small Hertfordshire club, the Lost Tribe, in June 1997.

descriptions: Outcasts aligned with Midlands Outlaws and joined Outlaws.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outcasts disbanded as a result of the conflict, aligning themselves with the Midlands Outlaws and joining the February 2000 'patch over' to the American Outlaws Association.

descriptions: Penose is allied with Surinamese cartels.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Albanian mafia , British firms , Colombian cartels , Moroccan mafia , 'Ndrangheta , Serbian mafia , Surinamese cartels , Triads , Turkish mafia , Cape Verdean mafia

descriptions: The Errol Flynns are the precursors to the 8 Mile sconys, meaning the 8 Mile sconys succeeded them.
edges.evidence_quote: The Errol Flynns are recognized as the precursors to most, if not all, Detroit gangs that followed in their wake. Some of the most notable successors include the "Be Like Boys", "Dexter Boys" (an offshoot of YBI), "Schoolcraft Boys or SCB's", "SNS", "Fenkell Boys", "FMK" ("Fenkell Mafia Killers"), "7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs", "Linwood Boys", "Brewster Boys", "Jeffries Boys", and "8 Mile sconys", ("JBFL") "Joy Boys" or "Exit 9"

descriptions: Ruthless Crip Gangsters attacked a MOD member's home.
edges.evidence_quote: Several members of the Ruthless Crip Gangsters drove to the Eau Claire home of a member of the gang Menace of Destruction. Three to five shots were fired at a man after a verbal confrontation.

descriptions: Cava was part of the Clan dei Quindiciari alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: I Cava, i Graziano ed altre famiglie criminali erano strette in un'alleanza che formava quello che, un tempo, era detto Clan dei Quindiciari.

descriptions: The Cava clan was part of the unified Clan dei Quindiciari together with the Graziano and other families.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan era già attivo durante gli anni Sessanta e, congiuntamente agli attuali rivali dei Cava e ad altre famiglie criminali, formava un unico gruppo camorristico, denominato "Clan dei Quindiciari".

descriptions: Graziano was part of the Clan dei Quindiciari alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: I Cava, i Graziano ed altre famiglie criminali erano strette in un'alleanza che formava quello che, un tempo, era detto Clan dei Quindiciari.

descriptions: The Graziano clan was part of the unified Clan dei Quindiciari together with the Cava and other families.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan era già attivo durante gli anni Sessanta e, congiuntamente agli attuali rivali dei Cava e ad altre famiglie criminali, formava un unico gruppo camorristico, denominato "Clan dei Quindiciari".

descriptions: Northern Brigade faction defected to SDF.
edges.evidence_quote: a faction of the Northern Brigade, which is a TFSA unit, defected to the SDF.

descriptions: The Nuova Camorra Speciale is a splinter group of NCO.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'Nuova Camorra Speciale' ... alla quale aderì anche il clan Belforte

descriptions: Most groups of the Nuova Camorra Speciale ended up gravitating around the Casalesi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: la maggior parte di tali gruppi finì col gravitare attorno al clan dei Casalesi

descriptions: The Belforte clan joined the Nuova Camorra Speciale.
edges.evidence_quote: alla quale aderì anche il clan Belforte

descriptions: The Nuova Camorra Speciale threatened the Vesuvian Camorra of Fabbrocino and Orefice.
edges.evidence_quote: insidia la camorra vesuviana di Fabbrocino e Orefice

descriptions: The Nuova Camorra Speciale had dialogue with the Mazzarella clan.
edges.evidence_quote: dialoga anche con l'altra sponda, quella dei Mazzarella

descriptions: The Nuova Camorra Speciale threatened the Vesuvian Camorra of Fabbrocino and Orefice.
edges.evidence_quote: insidia la camorra vesuviana di Fabbrocino e Orefice

Vineland Boys.description: Hispanic American street gang based in the San Fernando Valley, Los Angeles, founded in January 1988 by former 18th Street gang members. Named after Vineland Avenue, the gang has about 300 members and is part of the Sureño coalition under the Mexican Mafia. It has alliances with Armenian Power and the Abergil crime family, and ties to the Sinaloa Cartel. The gang was involved in the 2003 murder of Burbank Police Officer Matthew Pavelka, leading to a large-scale crackdown.

descriptions: Vineland Boys is a rival of 18th Street.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Avenues, Mara Salvatrucha, Playboys, Santa Monica 13, Temple Street (gang), Vineland Boys, Florencia 13, Bloods, Black P. Stones (Jungles)

descriptions: Abergil organization cooperated with Vineland Boys for drug supply and expansion.
edges.evidence_quote: US federal prosecutors claim that when the Abergils wished to expand their mob syndicate in the US, they turned for help to an upstart Latino street gang known as the Vineland Boys.

descriptions: Abergil crime family allied with Vineland Boyz street gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Vineland Boys

descriptions: Vineland Boys is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Vineland Boys

descriptions: Infobox lista a Vineland Boys como aliado.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Vineland Boys

descriptions: Sureños are allied with Vineland Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 18th Street gang, Armenian Power, Aryan Brotherhood, Avenues, El Monte Flores, Familia 27, Florencia 13, Forming Kaos, Gulf Cartel, Mexican Mafia, Mongols, Ontario Varrio Sur, Playboys, Puente 13, Santa Monica 13, Toonerville Rifa 13, Vineland Boys, White Fence, Temple Street (gang)

descriptions: Sureños are allied with Vineland Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Vineland Boys

descriptions: Toonerville Rifa 13 has a rivalry with Vineland Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Armenian Power , The Avenues , Westside Locos The Rascals, Frogtown Rifa , Vineland Boys , MS-13 , Russian mafia , Norte-14

descriptions: Vineland Boys was founded by former members of 18th Street gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland Boys gang was founded in [Sun Valley, Los Angeles] in January 1988 by former members and associates of the 18th Street gang.

descriptions: Vineland Boys formed an alliance with Abergil crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland Boys have formed alliances with Armenian Power and the Abergil crime family.

descriptions: Vineland Boys teamed up with Abergil crime family in 2000 for protection and cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang allegedly first teamed up with Abergils in 2000, offering protection and cooperation to the Israeli mafia's ecstasy trafficking ring operating in their territory.

descriptions: Vineland Boys formed an alliance with Armenian Power.
edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland Boys have formed alliances with Armenian Power and the Abergil crime family.

descriptions: Vineland Boys had a feud with the Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Boys' feud with the Mexican Mafia, known as "La Eme", sent the Boys looking for a new source of drugs, which the Israelis provided.

descriptions: Vineland Boys clashed with Mexican Mafia until 1998.
edges.evidence_quote: the Vineland Boys also clashed with the Mexican Mafia until the gang's founder Teddy Lopez y Alberto Ruiz was murdered by Mexican Mafia members at a Monterey Park nightclub in 1998.

descriptions: Vineland Boys joined the Mexican Mafia-controlled Sureño coalition after 1998.
edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland boys have since joined the Mexican Mafia-controlled Sureño gang coalition.

descriptions: Vineland Boys had ties to Sinaloa Cartel revealed in 2003.
edges.evidence_quote: revealing the Vineland Boys' ties to the Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Vineland Boys joined the Sureño coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland boys have since joined the Mexican Mafia-controlled Sureño gang coalition.

descriptions: Byrd Gang has Ghost Gang as a rival according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Ghost Gang Goonie Gang, Young Melph Mafia St Bernard Projects

descriptions: The Enterprise was created to secretly fund and supply the Contras.
edges.evidence_quote: With the third-party funds, North created an organization called The Enterprise, which served as the secret arm of the NSC staff and had its own airplanes, pilots, airfield, ship, operatives, and secret Swiss bank accounts.

descriptions: The Yugoslav People's Army supplied weapons and equipment to the Serb Volunteer Guard.
edges.evidence_quote: The SDG was the best equipped of the various para-military militias founded in the early 1990s, being supplied by the Yugoslav People's Army with guns, ammunition, lorries, light artillery, and shells.

descriptions: Los Linces was the shock group of Cártel de Juárez.
edges.evidence_quote: Era el grupo de choque del cártel compuesto por desertores de las fuerzas especiales del Ejército

descriptions: Barriox13 has conflicts with 135th St Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang has had conflicts with several rivals, including Piru gangs such as CPP, WSP, and 135th St Piru, although tensions have recently calmed.

'Ndrina Santaiti.description: The Santaiti are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Seminara, Calabria, Italy, also active in Liguria. They emerged as a dominant force in the 1970s after winning a feud against the Pellegrino clan. The clan is involved in various criminal activities and has been led by Carmine Demetrio Santaiti. They maintain alliances with other 'ndrine such as Brindisi and Caia.

descriptions: Gioffrè, Santaiti, and Romeo are the dominant 'ndrine of Seminara, acting together.
edges.evidence_quote: I Gioffrè , insieme ai Santaiti e ai Romeo, sono le 'ndrine egemoni di Seminara.

descriptions: Brindisi is listed as an allied 'ndrina in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: 'Ndrine alleate - Brindisi

descriptions: Caia is listed as an allied 'ndrina in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: 'Ndrine alleate - Caia

descriptions: Santaiti and Gioffrè are jointly the hegemonic 'ndrine of Seminara, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: I Santaiti , insieme ai Gioffrè , sono le 'ndrine egemoni di Seminara

descriptions: Santaiti won a feud against Pellegrino in the 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni '70 vincono la faida di Seminara contro la 'ndrina dei Pellegrino.

descriptions: Peshmerga is listed as a non-state opponent of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Non-state opponents: ... Peshmerga

descriptions: Peshmerga is listed as a non-state opponent of Islamic State in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Non-state opponents: ... Peshmerga

descriptions: Peshmerga is listed as an ally of PMF only against ISIS.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Peshmerga (only against ISIS)

descriptions: Peshmerga is listed as a non-state ally of PMF only against ISIS.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: State allies Iran, Iraq, Russia, Syria (until 2024), Non-state allies Islamic Resistance in Iraq, Hezbollah, Peshmerga (only against ISIS), Islamic Dawa Party (Nouri al-Maliki), Syrian Democratic Forces (only against ISIS), February 14 Youth Coalition, Houthis, Hezbollah Al-Hejaz, Sinjar Resistance Units

descriptions: MAG split from KA and later dissolved.
edges.evidence_quote: In August 1978, the majority split into two groups: KA focused on resuming interrupted legal and illegal activity, while the Marxist Workgroup (MAG) focused on analysing the organisational failure and dissolved in 1980.

'Ndrina Petullà.description: The Petullà are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cinquefrondi, Italy. Active since the 1980s, they are involved in international drug trafficking, with members in northern Italy, France, Germany, Peru, and Australia. They have been engaged in a feud with the Foriglio clan and have been targeted in multiple police operations.

descriptions: The Petullà and Foriglio were in a feud (faida) starting in 1987.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1987 viene ucciso il boss Raffaele Petullà e scoppia la, poi nominata, faida di Cinquefrondi con la 'ndrina dei Foriglio.

descriptions: The Petullà and Ladini were targeted together in the same operation, indicating association.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 15 dicembre 2015 vengono eseguiti 36 fermi, di cui 8 arresti, dalle forze dell'ordine che concludono l'operazione Saggio Compagno per traffico di stupefacenti, estorsione, porto d'armi illecito e associazione mafiosa contro presunti affiliati ai Petullà, i Foriglio e i Ladini.

descriptions: The Petullà (Ieraci-Petullà) and Squillace were arrested together in a joint operation, indicating cooperation in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 1º luglio 2008 termina l'operazione Alba e tramonto della polizia di Reggio Calabria e Polistena con 27 arresti di affiliati alle cosche Ieraci-Petullà di Cinquefrondi e Squillace di Polistena in collaborazione anche con appartenenti ai Nirta-Strangio.

descriptions: The Petullà (Ieraci-Petullà) collaborated with Nirta-Strangio in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 1º luglio 2008 termina l'operazione Alba e tramonto della polizia di Reggio Calabria e Polistena con 27 arresti di affiliati alle cosche Ieraci-Petullà di Cinquefrondi e Squillace di Polistena in collaborazione anche con appartenenti ai Nirta-Strangio.

descriptions: Ahfad al-Rasul Brigades supported protests against Ahrar al-Sham.
edges.evidence_quote: The Ahfad al-Rasul Brigades supported the protests against ISIL and Ahrar al-Sham in August 2013.

descriptions: Frogtown originally derived from Varrio White Fence.
edges.evidence_quote: gangs such as the Avenues (AVES), Frogtown (FTR), Hazard (BH), and other sections in the area originally derived from Varrio White Fence.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant – Yemen Province
descriptions: AQAP and ISIS-Y were in conflict but it ended by 2020.
edges.evidence_quote: AQAP's conflict with ISIS-Y had also dissipated in 2020, with no attacks being reported against either groups since February 2020.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant – Yemen Province
descriptions: AQAP and ISIS-Y are opponents.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Non-state opponents Islamic State Yemen Province

Organised crime in Nigeria.description: Organised crime in Nigeria encompasses fraud, banditry, drug trafficking, and racketeering, with groups operating across Western Africa and globally. Nigerian criminal gangs rose to prominence in the 1980s, often organized along familial and ethnic lines rather than a mafia model. Notable groups include the Area Boys in Lagos and confraternities like the Neo Black Movement of Africa, which have spread to Europe, Canada, and the United States.

descriptions: Camorra cooperates with Nigerian mafia in drug trafficking and prostitution.
edges.evidence_quote: I rapporti tra camorra e mafia nigeriana riguardano soprattutto il traffico di droga e la prostituzione.

descriptions: Hard Livings is allied with Nigerian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Nigerian mafia , Triads , Sicilian Mafia

descriptions: Black Axe is listed as an ally of the Nigerian mafia in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Black Axe

descriptions: Nigerian mafia is allied with Camorra in prostitution, drugs, and human trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: According to the police, the Nigerians are increasing their alliances with the Neapolitan Camorra and the Sicilian Mafia , the alliances are most regarded to prostitution, drugs and human trafficking business.

descriptions: Nigerian mafia pays tribute to Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Nigerian mafia pays tribute (a percentage of its profits from criminal rackets) to the Camorra and Sicilian Mafia.

descriptions: Nigerian mafia is in business with Camorra.
time_periods: tra la fine del XX secolo e l'inizio del XXI secolo
edges.evidence_quote: la mafia nigeriana (in affari con Cosa nostra e Camorra)

descriptions: Nigerian crime group allied with Capriati clan in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: In January 2025, the Italian police dismantled a drug trafficking scheme between a Nigerian crime group and the historic Capriati clan in the city of Bari in the region of Apulia .

descriptions: Nigerian crime group allied with Capriati clan in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: In January 2025, the Italian police dismantled a drug trafficking scheme between a Nigerian crime group and the historic Capriati clan in the city of Bari in the region of Apulia.

descriptions: Nigerian mafia is allied with Sicilian Mafia in prostitution, drugs, and human trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: According to the police, the Nigerians are increasing their alliances with the Neapolitan Camorra and the Sicilian Mafia , the alliances are most regarded to prostitution, drugs and human trafficking business.

descriptions: Nigerian mafia pays tribute to Sicilian Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Nigerian mafia pays tribute (a percentage of its profits from criminal rackets) to the Camorra and Sicilian Mafia.

time_periods: tra la fine del XX secolo e l'inizio del XXI secolo
edges.evidence_quote: la mafia nigeriana (in affari con Cosa nostra e Camorra)

descriptions: Nigerian mafia collaborated with Chinese triads in heroin trafficking in France.
edges.evidence_quote: The Nigerian mafia in France is involved in the sex trafficking of Nigerian women and girls, as well as heroin trafficking, in which they have collaborated with Chinese triads and other groups.

descriptions: Nigerian mafia cooperates with yakuza in heroin dealing and other crimes in Tokyo.
edges.evidence_quote: The Nigerian mafia in Tokyo engages in heroin dealing with the yakuza , the prostitution of Filipino women, money laundering, car theft and arranging fake marriages, amongst other crimes.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Africa : Nigerian mafia .

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Africa : Nigerian mafia .

descriptions: Sorianiello group had a pact with Nigerian individuals over drug debt, which turned into conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Secondo quanto accertato la spedizione punitiva venne architettata per colpire Oviamwonyi e un altro nigeriano Leo Uwadiae, con i quali i "Soraniello" avevano stipulato un patto: il pagamento di duemila euro per la restituzione della droga.

descriptions: Nigerian mafia is an ally of The Americans per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The YN's , 1400 EstateBoyz , Nigerian mafia , Triads

descriptions: Islamic Supreme Council of Iraq is part of the pro-Sistani faction within PMF.
edges.evidence_quote: According to Faleh A. Jabar and Renad Mansour, the pro-Sistani faction consists of those armed groups formed by Sistani's fatwa to defend Shia holy sites and by paramilitary of the Islamic Supreme Council of Iraq.

descriptions: Nuovo Ordine Sociale-Sole Nero is a splinter group of Nuovo Ordine Sociale.
edges.evidence_quote: splinter group of the Italian Atomwaffen "Nuovo Ordine Sociale-Sole Nero"

descriptions: Nuovo Ordine Sociale-Sole Nero was connected to Lions' Den.
edges.evidence_quote: the group was connected to jihadi terrorists. Allegedly a man from Bologna volunteered to fight in the Palestinian group Lions' Den

Solo Angeles.description: An outlaw motorcycle club formed in Tijuana, Mexico in 1959. The club's insignia is a chopper-style motorcycle. It conducts an annual charity run delivering toys to poor children in Tijuana. Despite being described as an outlaw club, its infobox labels it as 'NON OUTLAW ONLY SOCIAL'.

Baxter Street Dudes.description: The Baxter Street Dudes was a New York City teenage street gang active in the 1870s, consisting of former newsboys and bootblacks. Founded by Baby-Face Willie, they operated the Grand Duke's Theatre from a dive bar basement on Baxter Street in Manhattan. The gang wrote and performed plays and variety shows, using the theatre as a headquarters and underworld hangout. They engaged in tax evasion and thievery, and had rivals such as the Mulberry Blend.

descriptions: The infobox lists Mulberry Blend as a rival of the Baxter Street Dudes.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mulberry Blend

descriptions: Nigru is a branch of the Barbaro 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: Di questa famiglia esistono tre rami: quelli soprannominati "castani", quelli "nigri" e quelli "pillari" e 8 ndrine: Barbaro Castanu , Rosi , Nigru , Pillaru , Perre Maistru , Papalia Carciutu , Sergi 'Mbilli e i Trimboli Piseja .

descriptions: Barillaro and Franco were opposing sides in the Faida di Ciminà.
edges.evidence_quote: scoppia la cosiddetta faida di Ciminà in cui vengono coinvolte le famiglie Barillaro-Romano-Zucco da una parte, e i Varacalli-Franco-Polifroni-Spagnolo dall'altra

Baybaşin family.description: Kurdish crime syndicate founded around 1960 in Lice, Turkey by Mehmet Said Baybaşin. Initially involved in cannabis and opium cultivation, later expanded into heroin production and trafficking. Active in Turkey and Western Europe, known for strong family ties and involvement in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and murder. Currently led by Abdullah Baybaşin.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Baybaşin family as a rival of the Arifs.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Baybaşin family

descriptions: The Baybaşin family has rivalries with British firms.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: British firms , Turkish mafia

descriptions: The Baybaşin family commands and supports the Hackney Bombers.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hackney Bombers, a Kurdish gang in the United Kingdom under the command of Hüseyin Baybaşin, came to prominence following clashes with the Turkish-controlled Tottenham Turks gang. The Hackney Bombers, armed and financially supported by the Baybaşins, attacked a restaurant owned by Tottenham Turks with a group of masked thugs on motorbikes and AK-47s.

descriptions: The Baybaşin family is allied with the Kurdish mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Kurdistan Workers' Party , Kurdish mafia

descriptions: The Baybaşin family has rivalries with the Turkish mafia.

descriptions: The text speculates that Lola la Chata may have supplied heroin to the Beland family.
edges.evidence_quote: It is not known if the Beland family's supply of heroin came from Lola la Chata, a contemporary Mexican drug trafficker.

descriptions: Houthi Popular Committees are allies of the Houthis.

Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan.description: The Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan was a Sicilian Mafia clan based in Siculiana, Sicily, founded in the 1950s. It played a central role in international drug trafficking and money laundering for Cosa Nostra during the 1980s and 1990s, with operations extending to Canada and Venezuela. The clan was described by Italian prosecutors as an 'international holding' and was known for its tight family bonds.

descriptions: Cataldo cooperated with Cuntrera-Caruana in cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cataldo clan was also involved in cocaine trafficking from Colombia with the Sicilian Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan

descriptions: Mazzaferro conducted drug trafficking together with Cuntrera-Caruana in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Il commercio di stupefacenti negli anni '90 è stato fatto insieme ad alcune 'ndrine di Gioiosa Jonica e del clan siciliano agrigentino dei Cuntrera-Caruana.

descriptions: The clan supplied cocaine to 'Ndrangheta families.
edges.evidence_quote: They became seriously involved when a joint venture of 'Ndrangheta families needed Alfonso Caruana to supply them.

descriptions: The clan was involved in heroin trafficking with the Bonanno family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Italian police was following the movements of Giuseppe Bono, the middleman between the buyers of the Gambino and Bonanno crime families in New York and the Sicilian clans who organized the heroin traffic to the US.

descriptions: The infobox lists Cali Cartel as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cali Cartel

edges.evidence_quote: The Cuntrera-Caruana clan received half of the load, while John Gambino of the Gambino Family in New York City took care of the other 200 kilograms.

descriptions: The Gambino crime family and Cuntrera-Caruana clan shared a heroin shipment.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan received half of the load, while John Gambino took possession of the other 200 kilograms.

descriptions: Rizzuto family allied with Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan

descriptions: Cuntrera-Caruana clan brokered links between Solntsevskaya and Colombian cartels, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization is also involved in the international cocaine trade, with its links to Colombian drug cartels brokered by the Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan.

descriptions: Clan members are affiliated or aligned with the Popular Resistance Committees.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the clan are affiliated or aligned with various Palestinian and Islamist groups, including Fatah, Hamas, Popular Resistance Committees, and Al-Qaeda.

descriptions: PIJ and PRC jointly attacked Israel in March 2012.
edges.evidence_quote: PIJ and PRC opened attacks on Israel.

edges.source: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
descriptions: PFLP allied with Popular Resistance Committees.
edges.evidence_quote: The PFLP has therefore formed alliances with other leftist groups formed within the Palestinian Authority, including the Palestinian People's Party and the Popular Resistance Committees of Gaza.
